# CASE: tab-case-014-packages-api-v2-compatibility

> PWA adaptation to the new v2 packages API shape introduced by nex-case-032 (PR #216). The
> `GET /api/v2/tablet/packages` endpoint no longer returns `modifiers[]`; it returns
> `allowed_menus[]` with per-course limits and `base_price`. This case updates all PWA consumers
> to the new shape so the tablet does not silently break when PR #216 merges.

## Tier
2 — API consumer adaptation only. No schema change in the PWA; no new cross-app contract created
(this PR consumes the contract defined in nex-case-032). Touches 8 files across types, stores,
utils, components, and pages — breaking-change adaptation, not a new feature. Tier 2 per the triage
rubric (cross-app impact via consumed contract, no DB change in this repo).

## Branch
agent/tab-case-packages-api-v2

## Problem

`GET /api/v2/tablet/packages` changed shape in PR #216 (nex-case-032):

| Field | Old API | New API |
|---|---|---|
| Pricing | `price` | `base_price` |
| Meat selection | `modifiers[]` (individual POS items) | `allowed_menus[]` (menu categories) |
| Course limits | absent | `min_meat`/`max_meat`, `min_side`/`max_side`, `min_dessert`/`max_dessert`, `min_beverage`/`max_beverage` |
| Metadata | `accent`, `color`, `is_popular`, `is_taxable`, `tax`, `img_url`, `receipt_name`, etc. | **all dropped** |

The old PWA assumed `modifiers[]` of individual meat dishes (with `receipt_name` prefix
classification `P`/`B`/`C` → PORK/BEEF/CHICKEN). The new `allowed_menus[]` are Krypton menu
categories with an explicit `meat_category_code` field. The behavioral model change: the old
modifier browser showed individual dishes; the new inspector shows POS menu categories grouped by
`meat_category_code`.

Without this fix: package price shows ₱0, meat preview groups are empty, order submission may
send a stale package reference.

## Contrarian Review

**Verdict:** Proceed. Tier 2. Consumer-side adaptation — no new schema, no new contract.
Eight files need updating. The behavioral change in the inspector (categories instead of
individual items) is unavoidable given the API change. Backward-compat cast strategy (`as any`)
is acceptable for fields that were removed (is_taxable, tax) since those getters return 0/null,
which is the correct safe-default for the new Package shape.

## Success Criterion

Task is done when: `npm run test:run` passes all 414 tests (75 test files), `tsc --noEmit`
exits 0, and the PWA package card + inspector render from `allowed_menus[]` with correct
PORK/BEEF/CHICKEN grouping via `meat_category_code`.

## Root Cause

PR #216 (nex-case-032) is a breaking change to the `GET /api/v2/tablet/packages` response shape.
The PWA was built against the old `modifiers[]` shape and was not updated in the same PR.

## Proposed Fix

Update all 8 PWA files that reference the old shape:
1. `types/index.d.ts` — add `PackageAllowedMenu`; rewrite `Package` standalone
2. `stores/Menu.ts` — rewrite `normalizePackage()` + `extractModifierGroups()`
3. `utils/packageModifierGroups.ts` — add `groupAllowedMenusByCategoryCode`, `groupAllowedMenuPreviews`
4. `components/PackageCard.vue` — use `pkg.base_price`, `groupAllowedMenuPreviews`
5. `pages/order/packageSelection.vue` — rewrite inspector to use `allowed_menus[]`
6. `pages/order/in-session.vue` — `pkg.price` → `pkg.base_price` (packageTotal)
7. `components/order/CartSidebar.vue` — remove `tax.percentage` label reference
8. `tests/package-card-contract.spec.ts` — update assertion to new grouping helper

## Files Changed

- `types/index.d.ts` — added `PackageAllowedMenu` interface; rewrote `Package` (removed `extends MenuItem`, replaced `modifiers`/`accent`/`color`/`is_popular` with `allowed_menus`/`base_price`/course-limit fields)
- `stores/Menu.ts` — `normalizePackage()` adapted to `base_price` + `allowed_menus`; `extractModifierGroups()` adapted to `meat_category_code`
- `utils/packageModifierGroups.ts` — added `groupAllowedMenusByCategoryCode`, `groupAllowedMenuPreviews`, `PackageAllowedMenuGroup`, `PackageAllowedMenuPreviewGroup`; old exports retained for test compatibility
- `components/PackageCard.vue` — `pkg.price` → `pkg.base_price`; preview circles use `allowed_menus` grouped by category code; `is_popular` badge removed (field dropped)
- `pages/order/packageSelection.vue` — inspector rewritten from `Modifier` items to `PackageAllowedMenu` categories; `inspectorGroups`, `inspectorMenus`, `featuredMenu`, `selectFeaturedMenu`, `shiftFeaturedMenu` all updated; CSS class selectors preserved for contract spec
- `pages/order/in-session.vue` — `packageTotal` updated to `base_price`
- `components/order/CartSidebar.vue` — removed `tax.percentage` label from tax row
- `tests/package-card-contract.spec.ts` — updated assertion from old grouping helper name to new

Commits: `8173385` (types/stores/utils), `7245186` (components/pages/tests)

## Code Simplification

**Pass (code-simplifier, 2026-06-14):**

- `stores/Menu.ts` — `normalizePackage()`: removed 3 unnecessary `as any` casts on `pkg.base_price`
  and `pkg.allowed_menus` now that `Package` is properly typed with those fields. The
  `allowed_menus.map` callback parameter also dropped its `: any` annotation.
- `stores/Menu.ts` — `extractModifierGroups()`: removed `(pkg as any)?.` cast (direct field access
  on typed `Package`); removed `: any` from `.filter` and `.some` callbacks since
  `PackageAllowedMenu` carries all three accessed fields.
- `pages/order/packageSelection.vue` — removed dead `.pkg-touch-region` CSS rule (defined in
  `<style>` but never applied to any template element; the swipe handler infrastructure is
  intentionally retained per its inline comments).

**Confirmed clean (no change needed):**
- `types/index.d.ts` — new interfaces are tight; pre-existing commented-out block is out of scope.
- `utils/packageModifierGroups.ts` — old exports kept intentionally for test compat; `CATEGORY_CODE_TO_LABEL` duplication of `RECEIPT_PREFIX_TO_GROUP` is pre-existing, not from this task.
- `components/PackageCard.vue` — imports and computed refs all active.
- `pages/order/packageSelection.vue` (script) — swipe handlers and `currentIndex` retained intentionally per comments; no debug logs.
- `pages/order/in-session.vue` — `packageTotal` correctly reads `base_price` (case file "Risks" note was stale); no dead code.
- `components/order/CartSidebar.vue` — `orderSubtotal`/`orderTax` stubbed-at-zero with explanatory comments; used in template.
- `tests/package-card-contract.spec.ts` — clean, no stale imports.

**Hygiene (dead-code-cleanup):** PASS — no unused imports, no debug `console.*` calls, no temp files, no commented-out blocks introduced by this task.

## Remaining Risks

> [!warning] Must deploy after nex-case-032 (PR #216)
> The new PWA code references `allowed_menus[]` and `base_price`. If the PWA is deployed before
> PR #216 merges and migrates, the API still returns the old shape; `allowed_menus` will be
> undefined and the package card will render empty. The existing `normalizePackage()` guards
> (`Array.isArray(allowed_menus) ? ... : []`) make this fail gracefully rather than crash.

- After PR #216 merges to production, run `php artisan cache:clear` to bust the `tablet.packages.v2` cache.
